core_mechanism: A constant-rate Poisson point process of random events superimposed on a branching genealogical structure, yielding closed-form marginal probabilities of discrete outcome configurations that are then tested for parameter identifiability and for distinguishability between two competing generative processes
brief_reason: The abstract models transfer events as a Poisson process with a constant rate on a species/gene tree and derives gene-tree and site-pattern probabilities to assess identifiability and whether two event-generating processes can be told apart.
